label: Standing Bodyweight Squat
note: The default version; needs some standing balance.

label: Supported Squat (hold rail/counter)
note: Hold a stable surface — removes the balance demand.

label: Chair Sit-to-Stand
note: Stand up from / sit down to a chair. Chair gives support; very low-cog.

label: Wall Sit (isometric hold)
note: Static hold — hardest. Isometric holds raise blood pressure.

label: Seated Leg Extension
note: No standing or weight-bearing needed at all.

label: Standing Forward Fold
note: Head drops below heart — watch dizziness/BP.

label: Supported Hinge (hands on counter)
note: Hinge with support; head stays higher.

label: Seated Forward Fold (chair)
note: Reach toward the floor from a chair.

label: Seated Forward Fold (floor)
note: Legs extended on the floor; requires getting down to the floor.
